intro: Bulbophyllum lagaropetalum, also known as Lagaropetalum orchid, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which can aid in treating infections, wounds, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Bulbophyllum lagaropetalum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infused tea to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, and is also used to promote wound healing and skin care.
